events.text: The receiver's right exterior shell cracked against a rock wall during the retreat from Mondris. A solder joint inside the signal amplification module came loose.

{{characters.elias.name}} took the module out and reattached it with a fine solder needle he carried. That operation required steady hands and precise attention; when he worked on things like this, he'd go quiet inside. That quiet was his own choice, not forced.
When {{characters.alea.name}} sat down beside him, he didn't particularly notice. He was used to her choosing the spot with the windbreak wall—it really was better than other places.
The two sat like that. That silence wasn't awkward—it was the kind of silence where two people each doing their own thing shared the same space. Natural, needing no filling.

{{characters.alea.name}} opened her supply bag, took things out one by one, inventoried, categorized, confirmed quantities. She did that motion with practiced ease, no thinking required, her hands moved themselves.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: Thank you. At the supply station, you held off those soldiers, gave me time. Thank you.

Her hands paused. She glanced at him—he wasn't looking back, focused intently on that module. That thanks was spoken flatly, the way you voice something you thought you should say. No exaggeration, no performance, just saying it.

events.speaker: {{characters.alea.name}}
events.text: That was what I should have done.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: I know. But I still wanted to say thank you.
{{characters.alea.name}} put a medication pack into the sorted section, picked up the next item, let those two sentences settle in her mind.
His hands paused, then he laughed. That laugh was soft, a short sound from his throat.
{{characters.alea.name}} continued organizing supplies, said nothing more. {{characters.elias.name}} said nothing more. The two returned to that silence.
{{characters.alea.name}} finished the last item, pulled the supply bag closed, leaned against the wall, stretched her legs out, closed her eyes to rest.

{{characters.elias.name}} secured the solder joint, installed the module back, fastened the shell, confirmed the signal, stowed the receiver in his pocket. Didn't pull out anything else to disassemble.
Just sat like that, letting the wasteland wind blow through from outside.
